preamble: The palatalization of the voiceless alveolar sibilant /s/ into a voiceless palato-alveolar sibilant /ʃ/ (sh), typically triggered by following high front vowels or glides.

examples.from: PIE *s after r, u, k, i
examples.note: RUKI rule in Baltic: PIE *s → *š after resonants r/l, high vowels u/i, and velars k/g. E.g., PIE *wīsōs → Lithuanian 'viešas' (public); cf. Sanskrit RUKI rule showing same innovation.
Proto-Semitic lateral fricative *ś merged with [ʃ] in Hebrew; cf. Arabic شَمْس shams from the same root.
The phoneme /s/ is realized as a palatal sibilant before /i/.
